info.title: Privacy Automation - Data Subject Request (DSR) Automation
info.description: The Privacy Rights Automation are used to manage, process, and fulfill data subject access requests (DSARs), including request creation, subtask management, resolution codes, verification methods, and audit history.

hostname.description: The OneTrust hostname such as app.onetrust.com, app-eu.onetrust.com, app-de.onetrust.com, app-uk.onetrust.com, app-apac.onetrust.com, trial.onetrust.com, or uat.onetrust.com.

tags.name: Request Queues
tags.description: APIs used to create, search, update, and manage privacy request queues.

tags.name: Resolutions
tags.description: APIs used to create, update, delete, and retrieve resolution codes for privacy requests and subtasks.

tags.name: Results Summary
tags.description: APIs used to share results summaries.

tags.name: Subtasks
tags.description: APIs used to create, update, complete, and reprocess subtasks associated with privacy requests.

tags.name: Targeted Data Discovery
tags.description: APIs used to add, update, and retrieve structured or unstructured data discovery results linked to privacy requests.

tags.name: Verification Methods
tags.description: APIs used to create, update, and retrieve methods for verifying a data subject's identity.

get.description:
Use this API to retrieve a deletion certificate for a deleted request. The response will serve as the deletion certification and will include details such as the retention policy (if applicable), deletion date and time, and deletion mode.

> 🗒 Things to Know
>
> - Certificate data is available only for requests deleted within the past 360 days.

schema.description: Deleted Request Reference ID (10-character ID that is unique to each request)

post.description:
Use this API to share data discovery results summary with the data subject via email. The API supports sharing results in CSV or PDF format. When sharing as PDF, you must provide a report template ID to customize the output format.

> 🗒 Things to Know
>
> - The report template ID (not name) must be provided for PDF reports.
> - The API processes sharing asynchronously. For tracking, you can either mark a specific subtask as complete\n by providing the subtaskId (the request will auto-progress when the subtask is completed), or you can mark the entire request as complete using the markRequestAsComplete flag.
> - Comments in the request body support HTML content and will be sanitized.
> - The report name format is: `{YYYYMMD}_{RequestRefId}___Report` (e.g., `2025113_HMVBKRYTYE___Report`).

post.description: Use this API to search for requests by key terms, such as the data subject’s first name, last name, email address, or request ID.

schema.description:
A language code that identifies the language in which the request data should be searched, assuming translations are available for that language. 
Examples: en-us for English, de for German, fr for French, etc.

A date (yyyyMMdd). Include to retrieve a list of requests created on or after the specified date. For example, if you want to retrieve requests created on or after January 30, 2019, include the createddate=20190130 parameter in your query.
Results page you want to retrieve. Page number starts with 0 (0..N)
Number of records per page.

get.description:
Use this API to retrieve the creation details of a request by trace ID. The response will return the created date, creation status, and request ID.

> 👍
>
> For more information, see [Using the Asynchronous Request Creation API](https://my.onetrust.com/s/article/UUID-c35a2967-4869-e091-b957-f3819f20d55e?topicId=0TO1Q000000ItSgWAK).

get.description: Use this API to retrieve a list of all requests. The response will include details for each request along with the approver, date created, and data subject's first and last name.

schema.description:
A language code that identifies the language in which the request data should be returned, assuming translations are available for that language. 
Examples: en-us for English, de for German, fr for French, etc.

A date (yyyyMMdd). Include to retrieve a list of requests modified on or after the specified date. For example, if you want to retrieve requests modified on or after January 30, 2019, include the modifieddate=20190130 parameter in your query.

A date (yyyyMMdd). Include to retrieve a list of requests created on or after the specified date. For example, if you want to retrieve requests created on or after January 30, 2019, include the createddate=20190130 parameter in your query.
Filter requests by stage name. Parameters must be URL encoded.
A flag to exclude requests associated with user if they are not associated with specified organization.
Results page you want to retrieve. Page number starts with 0 (0..N)
Number of records per page. Maximum page size allowed is 500
Sorting criteria in the format: property(,asc|desc). Default sort order is ascending.

put.description:
Use this API to update the workflow stage for a specific request.

> 🗒 Things to Know
>
> - The following system default statuses can be passed as parameter values in the request when moving to a different stage: `NEW`, `VERIFYING_IDENTITY`, `IN_PROGRESS`, `REJECTED`, and `COMPLETE`.
> - The `resolutionTitle`, `comments`, and `isInternalComment` parameters are only used when the `nextStage` parameter is set to `REJECTED`.
> - You will not be able to advance the request to the next stage if the current stage has required subtasks that have not been completed.

> 👍 
> 
> For more information, see [Processing a Request](https://my.onetrust.com/s/article/UUID-f24b0959-dd7b-12f1-a475-7e2bd21292bb), [Closing a Request](https://my.onetrust.com/s/article/UUID-557ba7c1-1821-1e6a-9b8d-f846bcfb4515), and [Rejecting a Request](https://my.onetrust.com/s/article/UUID-347ac5f3-9b3f-7cbd-3b9c-36a9251e0e47).

put.description:
Use this API to pause the deadline of a request.

> 🗒 Things to Know
> 
> - The deadline will be removed from the request until it is resumed. Once resumed, it will be adjusted based on the time that the deadline was paused.
> - The request will be automatically resumed when the data subject responds. Manually resuming is also supported.

> 👍 
> 
> For more information, see [Pausing and Resuming a Request Deadline](https://my.onetrust.com/s/article/UUID-33144cf2-8696-e58b-20b2-9c87bb2a17db).

post.description:
Use this API to create a new request for the specified web form.

> 🗒 Things to Know
>
> - Visibility rules configured for the web form associated to the specified template will not be inherited after request creation. For more information, see [Configuring Dynamic Fields with Visibility Rules](https://my.onetrust.com/s/article/UUID-65f49274-b310-d0ef-397c-1d186871d920).

schema.description:
Every request is created against a webform template. The template ID is the GUID corresponding to a particular webform.
This can be obtained from the url by navigating to the webform.
e.g. in this url, 'https://trial.onetrust.com/app/#/pia/dsar/webform/manage/6519ffd5-fe6b-4804-8dfc-1c2ae2da212d',
'6519ffd5-fe6b-4804-8dfc-1c2ae2da212d' is the templateId.

get.description:
> ❗️ End of Support Notification
>
> As of January 1, 2026, this API is now deprecated. Any customers leveraging this API are encouraged to use the [Get List of Subtasks by Criteria API](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/getallsubtasksv3usingpost). For more information, see [OneTrust API Sunsetting & Deprecation Guidelines](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/onetrust-api-sunsetting-deprecation-guidelines).

Use this API to retrieve a list of all subtasks. The response will include details for each subtask along with the request approver, created date, and deadline.

> 🗒 Things to Know
> 
> - This API does not return subtasks in **Complete** status.

schema.description:
A language code that identifies the language in which the Request Type and Subject Type values should be returned, assuming translations are available for that language. 
Examples: language=en-us for English, language=de for German, language=es for Spanish, etc.

A date (yyyyMMdd). Include to retrieve a list of subtasks modified on or after the specified date. For example, if you want to retrieve subtasks modified on or after January 30, 2019, include the modifieddate=20190130 parameter in your query.

To get a SubTasks according to Status.
Examples: status=assigned
An optional parameter to show subtasks on open requests if set to true.if this parameter is set to false or omitted, the response will contain subtasks on all requests Examples: openrequests=true
Results page you want to retrieve. Page number starts with 0 (0..N)
Number of records per page. Maximum page size allowed is 500
Sorting criteria in the format: property(,asc|desc). Default sort order is ascending.

Too Many Requests.

For more information, see [API Rate Limits](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/rate-limits-overview).

schema.description: The number of seconds after which requests will be allowed again.
The unit of time for which the rate limit applies
The unique identifier for the rate-limiting event.
The number of requests made within the specified period.
The number of requests allowed within the specified period.

put.description: Use this API to resolve an existing subtask, marking it as Complete.

schema.description: Sub-Task UUID

Too Many Requests.

schema.description: The number of seconds after which requests will be allowed again.

put.description:
Use this API to reprocess a system subtask.

> 🗒 Things to Know
>
> - System subtasks in the **Complete** and **Ready for Review** statuses are not supported.

schema.description: Subtask ID

429.description:
Too Many Requests. 
For more information, see [API Rate Limits](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/rate-limits-overview).

schema.description: The number of seconds after which requests will be allowed again.
The unit of time for which the rate limit applies
The unique identifier for the rate-limiting event.
The number of requests made within the specified period.
The number of requests allowed within the specified period.

put.description:
Use this API to update the status of an existing subtask.

> 🗒 Things to Know
>
> - The following statuses can be passed as parameter values in the request: `IN_PROGRESS`, `FAILED`, `COMPLETED`, and `REJECTED`.
> - The Resolution ID can only be passed if the subtask status is updated to `COMPLETED` or `REJECTED`.

schema.description: Subtask Id

post.summary: Get List of Subtasks by Criteria
post.description: Use this API to retrieve a list of all subtasks by filter criteria.

schema.description: Include closed subtasks in the response
Only include activated subtasks in the response
Results page you want to retrieve. Page number starts with 0 (0..N)
Number of records per page. Maximum page size allowed is 500
Sorting criteria in the format: property(,asc|desc). Default sort order is subTaskId ascending.

comment.description: Comments can be updated for system subtask status call - this can be used for reason for failure. Longer comments may impact system performance.
comment.example: Updating the status for this reason

status.description: Status to be updated to completed or failed

resolutionId.description: Resolution ID (can only be passed if the subtask status is updated to COMPLETED or REJECTED)

userEmail.description: Email of the user associated with the subtask

description.example: This subtask is for data discovery

assignedTogroup.description: Whether the subtask is assigned to a group

groupName.description: Name of the group if assigned to a group

Comment.description: Internal comment to identify reason for reprocessing the subtask. Longer comments may impact system performance.

comment.description: Comment for the subtask completion. Longer comments may impact system performance.

markAsComplete.description: Whether to mark the subtask as complete

attachments.description: List of attachments for the subtask

id.description: The unique identifier for the resolution code. This is automatically generated when a new resolution code is created, not required in resolution creation payload.

close.description: Indicates whether this resolution code should be available as an option when closing a request. Set to true to make it available for closing requests.

reject.description: Indicates whether this resolution code should be available as an option when rejecting a request. Set to true to make it available for rejecting requests.

complete.description: Indicates whether this resolution code should be available as an option when completing a request. Set to true to make it available for completing requests.

type.description: Specifies the type of resolution code, which determines where it appears in the UI. By default, this is set to 10 for request-level resolutions. Use 20 for subtask resolutions.

titleTranslations.description: A map of language codes to translated titles for this resolution code. English (en-us) translation is required. Each translation must be unique per language and type across all resolution codes.

descriptionTranslations.description: A map of language codes to translated descriptions for this resolution code. The default English (en-us) translation must be present.

id.description: The unique identifier for this comment.

verificationMethodId.description: The identifier of the verification method this comment belongs to.

comment.description: The text content of the comment providing details about the verification process.
comment.example: Passport verified with photo matching the requester's profile picture.

lastModifiedDate.description: The timestamp when this comment was last modified.

type.description: The type of comment, represented as an integer code.

attachments.description: A list of attachments associated with this comment, such as scanned documents or screenshots used in the verification process.

id.description: The unique identifier for this verification method.

verificationMethodName.description: The name of the verification method used to verify the data subject's identity.

description.description: A detailed description of how the verification method was applied.
description.example: Verified government-issued photo ID matched with requester information.

verificationStatus.description: The current status of the verification process.

verificationComments.description: A list of comments and attachments associated with this verification method. This field is only populated when includeComments is set to true in the request.

verificationType.description: The type of verification process used.

createdDate.description: The timestamp when this verification method was created.

lastModifiedDate.description: The timestamp when this verification method was last modified.

pauseDeadline.description: If PauseDeadline is marked true, the deadline will be paused. If PauseDeadline is marked false, the deadline will be resumed.

comment.description: Internal comment to be added on pause or resume.

language.description:
This is the ISO language code for OneTrust supported languages.

The language code provided here informs the language of the comment.

Examples: en-us for English, de for German, fr for French, etc

requestQueueId.description: The 10-character unique identifier of the request.

currentStage.description: The name of the current workflow stage.

statusCode.description: The numeric identifier for the resolution code.

nextStage.description: The name of the workflow stage to which the request will be moved.

resolutionTitle.description: The `titleTranslation` parameter value of the resolution. This value should be the translation that corresponds to the language code defined in the `language` parameter of this payload, if available. This value is obtained using the [Get List of Resolutions API](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/getallv2resolutionsusingget). This parameter is only used when the `nextStage` parameter is set to `REJECTED`.

comments.description: The comments to add when rejecting the request. This parameter is only used when the `nextStage` parameter is set to `REJECTED`.

isInternalComment.description: This flag indicates whether the comment added when rejecting a request is for internal use only and will not appear in the Privacy Portal.

comment.description: A comment to accompany the current action of linking/ unlinking requests

requestQueueRefId.description: Reference ID of the request. This is a 10-character ID that is unique to each request.

firstName.description: First name of the data subject.

lastName.description: Last name of the data subject.

organization.description: Organization to which this request is assigned.

status.description: Current stage of the request. Various stages in the order of progression are: New, Verifying identity, In progress, Rejected, Complete.

requestTypes.description: Request types selected while submitting the request.

isExtended.description: Returns true if the request complete time is being extended.

dateCreated.description: Date on which request is submitted.

dateUpdated.description: Last activity date on the request.

subjectTypes.description: Subject types selected while submitting the request. Subject type can be: Prospective Employee, Student, Customer, Contractor, Employee, Patient.

approver.description: Approver or assignee for the request.

language.description: Language in which the request is created.

countryCode.description: The code for the country as per ISO 3166. e.g. US for the United States, DE for Germany.

email.description: Email provided while submitting the request.

dateCompleted.description: Date on which the request is completed.

resolution.description: Resolution selected if the request is rejected.

remainingDaysForMaxDeadline.description: Legal Deadline + Maximum Number of Extension Days based on country of request + Total Pause days.

maxDeadlineExtensionDate.description: Legal Deadline + Maximum Number of Extension Days based on country of request + Total Pause days.

assignedToGroup.description: Indicates if the request reviewer is assigned to a usergroup or not.

additionalStatuses.description: Status of the request timer, such as PAUSED or ACTIVE.

internalComment.description: Comments on the request can be internal or public. This flag determines if the comment is internal or public.

language.description:
This is the ISO language code for OneTrust supported languages.

The language code provided here informs the language of the comment.

Examples: en-us for English, de for German, fr for French, etc

requestsDeleted.description: Number of requests which are deleted in the API call (up to a maximum of 50 requests).

requestsPending.description: Number of requests which match the filter criteria which have not been deleted in this call.API should be called again to delete additional requests.

StartDate.description: Start date for filter, provide in yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z' format

EndDate.description: End date for filter, provide in yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z' format

RequestIDs.description: An array of 10-character Unique IDs of the Request(s) to be deleted.

CreatedDateRange.description: A date range for outlining start date and end date. All requests with a Created Date within this range will be deleted.

EndDateRange.description: A date range for outlining start date and end date. All requests with an End Date within this range will be deleted.

ModifiedDateRange.description: A date range for outlining start date and end date. All requests with a Modified Date within this range will be deleted.

Status.description: Filter requests by the Stage name. Only requests in this stage will be deleted.

Organization.description: Filter requests by Organization. Only requests in this organization will be deleted.

Country.description: Filter requests by Country code. Only requests with this Country selected will be deleted.

WorkflowID.description: Filter requests by Workflow ID. Only requests using this workflow will be deleted.

WebformID.description: Filter requests by Web Form ID. Only requests using this web form will be deleted.

RequestType.description: Filter requests by Request Type. Only requests with the Request Type will be deleted.

SubjectType.description: Filter requests by Subject Type. Only requests with the Subject Type will be deleted.

assignedToGroup.description: Indicates whether the subtask is assigned to a group rather than an individual.

requestApprover.description: The name of the person who approved the parent request.

requestOrgName.description: The organization name associated with the request.

requestQueueDateCreated.description: The date when the parent request was created.

requestQueueDeadline.description: The deadline for the parent request.

requesterEmail.description: The email address of the requester

subTaskAssignee.description: The person assigned to the subtask

subTaskCreatedDate.description: The date when the subtask was created

subTaskDescription.example: Discover all data related to the subject

subTaskLastUpdatedDate.description: The date when the subtask was last updated

subTaskReminderDate.description: The reminder date for the subtask

subTaskRequired.description: Indicates whether the subtask is required

subTaskStatus.description: The current status of the subtask

subTaskResolution.description: The resolution code for the subtask if completed or rejected

subjectTypes.description: Subject types of the parent request

currentRequestStage.description: The current stage of the parent request

subtaskCreatedStage.description: The stage when the subtask was created

RequestType.description: Filter subtasks by Request Type. Only subtasks belonging to requests with this Request Type will be retrieved.

SubjectType.description: Filter subtasks by Subject Type. Only subtasks belonging to requests with this Subject Type will be retrieved.

Organization.description: Filter subtasks by Organization. Subtasks in this organization and related child organizations will be retrieved if includeChildOrgs is true.

SystemName.description: Filter subtasks by integration system name. Only system subtasks associated with this system will be returned.

items.description: Filter subtasks by their current status. You can provide multiple status values to include subtasks in any of those statuses.

SubtaskType.description: Filter subtasks by type - either 'manual' for user-created subtasks or 'system' for automated system subtasks.

OpenRequests.description: When set to true, only subtasks belonging to open requests will be included in the results. If false or omitted, subtasks from both open and closed requests will be returned.

SubtaskDateCreated.description: Filter subtasks by their creation date. Only subtasks created within this date range will be included in the results. Use ISO 8601 format.

SubtaskDateUpdated.description: Filter subtasks by their last update date. Only subtasks updated within this date range will be included in the results. Use ISO 8601 format.

RequestDeadline.description: A date range for outlining start date and end date. All subtasks with a Request Deadline within this range will be retrieved. Accepted date format: YYYY-MM-DDT00:HH:MM.SSSZ

RequestDateCreated.description: A date range for outlining start date and end date. All subtasks with a Request Created Date within this range will be retrieved. Accepted date format: YYYY-MM-DDT00:HH:MM.SSSZ

currentRequestStage.description: Filter subtasks by current request stage. Accepted values include: All default and custom request stages are supported.

subtaskCreatedStage.description: Filter subtasks by subtask created stage. Accepted values include: All default and custom request stages are supported.

language.description: Specifies the language code for localized field values in the response. Translations will be provided for this language if available.

IncludeChildOrgs.description: By default set to true if omitted. On true Subtasks of Child Organizations of the given Organization will be included.On False Subtasks of the given Organization will be included. Effective only if 'organization' value is provided.

ExactSystemNameMatch.description: When set to true, performs exact match for SystemName instead of substring matching. This ensures only subtasks from systems with exact name match are returned. By default set to false for backward compatibility.

system.description: Name or identifier of the system from which this data is sourced. This will be displayed on the Results Summary.

results.description: Dataset that includes list of data groups with nested name-value-pairs to capture structured data.

attachments.description: Optional. List of name-value-pairs containing file names and IDs to attach. Use the Add File API to add file into OneTrust and retrieve the File IDs. These files will be visible in the Results Summary.

type.description: The type of unstructured data (e.g., document, image, email).

status.description: The processing status of the unstructured data.

key.description: A unique identifier or name for the unstructured data item.

value.description: The content or reference to the unstructured data.

isRedact.description: Indicates whether the data should be redacted in the response.

attributes.description: Additional attributes or metadata associated with the unstructured data.

language.description: The ISO language code for OneTrust supported languages. Examples: en-us for English, de for German, fr for French, etc.

additionalData.description: Additional fields in key value format, required for request processing. These could be webform or non webform fields

requestTypes.description: Respondents privacy request type. This should match with the webform subject type key, key values are part for webform submission payload.

subjectTypes.description: Respondents subject type or relation ship with tenant. This should match with the webform subject type key, key values are part for webform submission payload.

requestTraceId.description: Unique trace id for request. This can be used to avoid creating duplicate request.

commentRequired.description: Whether a comment is required when completing the subtask

resolutionRequired.description: Whether a resolution is required when completing the subtask

templateName.description: Name of the subtask template to use.

comment.description: Comment to include in the shared report. Supports HTML content.

markRequestAsComplete.description: Flag to mark the request as complete when sharing results

subtaskId.description: Optional subtask ID that will be marked complete after the results summary share finishes successfully.

reportType.description: Type of report to generate. Use 'CSV' for structured data export or 'PDF' for formatted report with template.

CsvResultsSummaryShareRequest.title: Share results summary in CSV format

reportTemplate.description: Template ID (UUID) for PDF report generation. This is required for PDF exports.

PdfResultsSummaryShareRequest.title: Share results summary in PDF format

description.description: The description of the request attribute audited.

fieldName.description: The name of the request attribute audited.

description.description: The description of the request attribute audited.

changeDateTime.description: The timestamp when the change was done.

recordedDateTime.description: The timestamp when the audit was recorded.

requestQueueRefId.description: Reference ID of the request. This is a 10-character ID that is unique to each request.

lastName.description: Last name of the data subject.

dateCreated.description: Date on which request is submitted.

subjectTypes.description: Subject types selected while submitting the request. Subject type can be: Prospective Employee, Student, Customer, Contractor, Employee, Patient.

approver.description: Approver or assignee for the request.

language.description: Language in which the request is created.

countryCode.description: The code for the country as per ISO 3166. e.g. US for the United States, DE for Germany.

email.description: Email provided while submitting the request.

dateCompleted.description: Date on which the request is completed.

resolution.description: Resolution selected if the request is rejected.

remainingDaysForMaxDeadline.description: Legal Deadline + Maximum Number of Extension Days based on country of request + Total Pause days.

maxDeadlineExtensionDate.description: Legal Deadline + Maximum Number of Extension Days based on country of request + Total Pause days.

assignedToGroup.description: Indicates if the request reviewer is assigned to a usergroup or not.

additionalStatuses.description: Status of the request timer, such as PAUSED or ACTIVE.

daysToRespond.description: Number of days to respond to the request.

workflowID.description: ID of the workflow associated with the request.

totalCost.description: Total cost associated with the request.

additionalData.description: Both "additionalData" and "requestAdditionalData" hold identical information. Opt for "requestAdditionalData" for improved parsing and to prevent issues related to Unicode characters.

requestV2CommentDtos.description: List of comments associated with the request.

requestAdditionalData.description: The "requestAdditionalData" can include both the fields from a Webform Template and query parameters that are provided through the Webform URL when creating or updating a request.

requestRefId.description: Reference ID of the request. This is a 10-character ID that is unique to each request.

retentionPolicy.description: The name of the retention policy that deleted the request. Null if deletion mode is not RETENTION_POLICY. If the policy is deleted, this will show the policy ID.

deletionDate.description: Timestamp when the request was deleted.

userId.description: The ID of the user who manually deleted the request. This is null if the deletion was not manual.

groupOrder.description: Order in which group has been created.

source.description: Name of the system where the attachment originated and displayed as targeted data discovery system for files added by user.

fileSize.description: Size of the file in kilobyte.

fieldName.description: The name for a data field in the results summary

value.description: This is the actual data element.

system.description: Displays the name of the source of a particular data element.

scopes.DSAR_READ: Read-only access to DSAR objects scope for external systems
scopes.DSAR_WRITE: Access to DSAR objects scope for external systems
